author: @swisswildpicsswp3095
text:
Hi! How is this project going?
I'm looking to build a pedal powered compressor myself, but I'd like to see a proof of concept beforehand. My main goal is painting in remote places with no electrical power (I have a very long fence). A typical small workshop compressor needs at least 1kW to operate a spray gun at 2 bars. The average cyclist can outpout 200W continuously. 
With a fully loaded 50l tank (8 bars max pressure) and a continuously pedaling friend, for how long could I operate my spray gun?

author: @bobciecko290
text: Replacing stored electricity  with  stored compressed air is on my mind.  Can we use a windmill  or PV solar to steadily accumulate air pressure  up to about 100 psi in large tanks for periodic use such as a pneumatic motor driving a pontoon boat?  The large tanks are already there for flotation anyway and removing batteries from the picture is a very green idea..

text:
I saw in a video where an old compressor that was 30 years old exploded because the tank rusted out.

So the guy in the video worn that to train the tank completely and I suspect repainting the inside of the tank with a rust converting chemical that doesn't eat through the metal but converts the rust to a non-rusting material.

author: @Thunderstormworld
text: Robert I work on farms and there's plenty compressors which the motors are burnt-out. I've suggested to the farmers that why don't they connect a vertical turbine to the compressor so each time the wind blows (which in my area is quite a lot) would keep their electrical compressors topped up and stop them from switching on and off every now and then, would save a lot of electricity too in the long run.

author: @videomentaryproductionschannel
text: Yah great Idea, but I'm thinking you need a much bigger flywheel,  so you get more energy yes I know that when you start pedaling it will be hard, but once your up to speed it will take very little effort to keep it going, because the flywheel will be doing most of the work, also as you say you need to change the gearing, to get it up to the initial pressure,  then change Down gears to give you more power, but I would say you must make that flywheel much more heavy and bigger if possible,  years ago when I was 15, I used a fly-press to nock out 1,000s of brackets, and that had a flywheel on it, I could do this job for hours with out getting tired 😫, but it worked so well,  in fact it was faster than a Hydraulic press that replaced it, in the end my governor changed it back to the flywheel kind 😆, another great video, can't wait to see you upgrading it 😀

author: @AdrianP247
text:
Could you use the dynapod as a pump? 
Then set up a "Tromp" water air compression system. Pump the water to the high top-end of the trompe water inlet. then in the middle of the tromp (where the compressed air comes out) connect it to the storage tank. The excess water can be "recycled" into the pump for another go around.
You would then be substituting the compressor and the motor parts with yourself, the dynapod and the tromp pipes...

author: @nostradormouse6122

This is heroically Heath Robinson. You are the Avatar of the British Engineering tradition of identifying the interesting problems.

If I didn't think you'd end up begging for coppers in a Whitehall corridor, I'd put you forward for promotion within the system myself.

replies.text: considering it takes more resources then it returns its pretty much a waste of time.    :)

replies.author: @Web3Dre
replies.text: @carmichaelmoritz8662 If you mean that it takes more energy than it outputs. Then yes of course it does. If not it would be an over unity device. And those don't exists "yet" :)

replies.author: @carmichaelmoritz8662
replies.text: @Web3Dre well that all depends. we eat resources and in certain cases we get more out then we put in .  lets say i eat to create waste , then turn that waste into some sort of fuel and in return get more energy out then i used while using some other sort of device to power the peddle powered air compressor.  or just make a explosion type air compressor   :)   many different ways to compress air.

replies.author: @Web3Dre
replies.text: @carmichaelmoritz8662 Oh, you mean the conversion of energy and the use of pedaling as a way to generate power? In that case I do agree, there are far better ways to generate energy. But as a storage device I think it was a good idea. Since the energy could be stored for years without any losses. Even though one could perhaps argue that the same volume filled with gasoline would have a higher energy density than compressed air. I think the video was trying to show an alternative way of storing energy :)

author: @azlandpilotcar4450
text: Someone I know does building projects with the U.S. Amish community.  Some of those good people have no religious compunction against using compressed air for power, just not electricity.  There's an entire, small, loaed industry based on converting farm and household appliances for use by those small, communities.  You'd once started down that path with wind or water powered air compressors, glad that you've come back to it. Well done.
